must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation

must_detect.matched_text:
From: Ms. Liao Wen, Chief Compliance Officer, Yunlan Harbor Renewables Co., Ltd. (USCC: 9X1A8QZ7L3P6D2R5Y) — contact: compliance@yunlanharbor.cn
Subject: MNPI controls, PIPL/CAC considerations, and pre-IPO safeguards for prospective STAR Market listing

1) MNPI: A non-public cyber incident occurred on 23 May; ransomware affected OT systems at the Nanting wind farm SCADA for 11 hours; root-cause and loss estimates are preliminary and not yet disclosed to the market or CSRC.
2) Crypto/DPT adjacency: Our subsidiary Shenzhen LumenChain Technology Co., Ltd. is drafting an internal whitepaper for a utility token pilot to settle carbon-credit transfers on a permissioned chain; no regulatory filings have been made with PBoC/CSRC and this remains inside information.
3) ESG/climate: Draft restatement of Scope 2/3 data and a contemplated RMB 600m sustainability-linked bond include a KPI-linked step-up of 25 bps and early redemption features; these commercial terms are in negotiation and are non-public.
4) Insider list: Update and circulate the insider list per CSRC Administrative Measures on Information Disclosure; include Ms. Liao Wen, Mr. Zhao He (CIO), Ms. Tang Rui (Treasury), and external counsel; store resident ID numbers as masked fields and avoid collecting minors’ (<14) information per PIPL.
5) Information barriers: Research and investment teams must remain on separate floors and systems; use a clean team for data rooms; diligence calls are to be chaperoned; no forwarding of cyber reports or token drafts outside the restricted “Seabird” workspace.
6) Blackout window: Effective immediately, impose a trading blackout for all designated insiders and directors until public announcement and, for equity-linked instruments, until CSRC acceptance is posted on the exchange website; suspend ESPP transactions for affected employees.
7) Tipping/selective disclosure: Vendors (SOC, cloud) and counterparties must be briefed not to discuss the incident or token pilot; any inquiries from analysts are to be directed to IR; no off-the-record briefings or sharing of draft KPI targets.
8) Cross-border transfer: The incident log contains employee device identifiers and emails; proposed sharing with a Singapore-based SOC requires CAC security assessment or the Standard Contract, plus PIPL consent notices; do not export facial images, resident ID scans, or salary data.
9) HR workflow: For onboarding and disciplinary reviews, limit collection to necessary employment data; store USCC copies for counterparties but only retain employee resident ID numbers in hashed form; no cross-border HR transfer without CAC filing.
10) Education/marketing (benign): The sustainability team will host an ESG webinar explaining climate risk metrics and a demo carbon ledger; materials use synthetic data and do not reference any issuer names, token economics, or commercial terms.
11) Operations-only (benign): IT will run a phishing simulation and a tabletop incident drill; these are training exercises and do not indicate any current or future incident severity.
12) Financing context: A draft term sheet with Haiyu Capital for a pre-IPO Series D contemplates a price floor of RMB 18.50 per share and an earn-out tied to CSRC registration milestones; NDA is executed, and documents must remain within the Seabird restricted drive.
